name: Integration, Balance & Performance
theme: 60fps optimization, game feel verification, full playthrough validation, save/load integrity, collectible accuracy, boss balance, no softlocks
features: All features — integration and polish pass

criteria.description: The game loads without any JavaScript errors. index.html loads all 18 script files correctly, Game.init() completes, and the title screen renders.
testSteps: Start dev server (python3 -m http.server 8080)
Navigate to http://localhost:8080 in Playwright
Wait for canvas to be visible
Check browser console for any JavaScript errors during load
Verify title screen text 'Kingdoms of the Canvas' or 'Press Enter' is rendered
criteria.threshold: Game loads with canvas visible, title screen renders, zero JS errors in console on load

criteria.description: All 13 stages defined in WorldMap.STAGES are loadable. Level.loadStage(stageId) succeeds for every stage ID (1-1 through 5-1) without throwing errors.
testSteps: For each, call GameState.currentStageId = id; Level.loadStage(id)
Verify Level.width > 0 and Level.height > 0 after each load
Verify no console errors thrown during any stage load
criteria.threshold: All 13 stages load successfully with width > 0 and height > 0, no errors thrown

criteria.description: Every stage has a valid spawn point and exit point. Level.spawnX, Level.spawnY, Level.exitX, Level.exitY are all set to non-zero values for each stage.
testSteps: For each of the 13 stage IDs, load the stage via Level.loadStage(id)
Check that Level.spawnX > 0 and Level.spawnY > 0
Check that Level.exitX > 0 and Level.exitY > 0
Verify spawn point is not inside a solid tile
Verify exit point is reachable (exitX > spawnX for side-scrolling levels)
criteria.threshold: All 13 stages have non-zero spawn and exit positions, spawn is not inside solid geometry

criteria.description: Every stage has boss data defined. Level.bossData is non-null and Level.bossArenaX > 0 for all 13 stages.
testSteps: For each of the 13 stage IDs, load the stage via Level.loadStage(id)
Check that Level.bossData is not null and not undefined
Check that Level.bossData.type is a non-empty string
Check that Level.bossData.spawnX > 0 and Level.bossData.spawnY > 0
Check that Level.bossArenaX > 0
criteria.threshold: All 13 stages have bossData with valid type and spawn coordinates, bossArenaX > 0

criteria.description: Every stage has coin collectibles placed. Level.coinPositions is a non-empty array for all 13 stages, and the count matches or exceeds the totalCoins defined in WorldMap.STAGES.
testSteps: For each of the 13 stage IDs, load the stage
Verify Level.coinPositions is an array with length > 0
Compare Level.coinPositions.length to the corresponding WorldMap.STAGES entry's totalCoins
The coin count should be >= totalCoins (some coins may be placed beyond the tracked total)
criteria.threshold: All 13 stages have coin positions, and coinPositions.length >= totalCoins from WorldMap.STAGES for each stage

criteria.description: Every stage has enemy spawn data. Level.enemySpawns is a non-empty array for all 13 stages, with each entry having a valid type, x, and y.
testSteps: For each of the 13 stage IDs, load the stage
Verify Level.enemySpawns is an array with length > 0
For each spawn entry, verify it has a 'type' string, numeric 'x', and numeric 'y'
Verify enemy types match known types from Enemies system
criteria.threshold: All 13 stages have at least 1 enemy spawn with valid type/x/y data

criteria.description: World map linear unlock progression works correctly. Initially only stage 1-1 is unlocked. Completing stage 1-1 unlocks 1-2, completing 1-2 unlocks 1-3, and so on through 5-1.
testSteps: Clear save data via SaveSystem.clearSave()
Call WorldMap.init() and check that only node 0 (stage 1-1) is unlocked
Simulate completing stage 1-1: SaveSystem.recordStageCompletion('1-1', 30, 10)
Re-init WorldMap and verify stage 1-2 (node 1) is now unlocked
Repeat for at least 3 more stages to verify chain progression
criteria.threshold: Completing a stage unlocks the next stage in sequence; initially only 1-1 is unlocked

criteria.description: No softlocks: the game can transition from STAGE to STAGE_COMPLETE when the player reaches the exit zone after defeating the boss. The boss-defeat-to-exit flow works for at least one stage per world.
testSteps: Inspect _checkExitZone() in states.js — verify it checks that Enemies.boss is null (boss defeated) before allowing exit
Inspect _checkBossArena() — verify boss arena wall placement does not block the exit path
Verify that boss defeat (Enemies.boss set to null) properly enables exit zone checking
Verify that the transition to STAGE_COMPLETE calls setupStageComplete which calls WorldMap.completeStage
criteria.threshold: Exit zone only activates after boss is defeated; boss arena walls don't block exit; complete flow from boss defeat to stage complete is unbroken

criteria.description: Victory state is reachable. Completing the final stage (5-1, The Citadel) triggers the VICTORY state with the victory screen showing total time, total coins, and death count.
testSteps: Search for VICTORY state handling in states.js and menu.js
Verify that stage 5-1 completion triggers GameState.transitionTo(VICTORY) or equivalent
Verify setupVictory() calculates totalTime, totalCoins, and totalDeaths from save data
Verify Menu.initVictory and Menu.renderVictory exist and display stats
criteria.threshold: Citadel completion triggers VICTORY state; victory screen shows time, coins, and deaths

criteria.description: Save system persists completed stages, coin records, and best times across page reloads. SaveSystem.recordStageCompletion stores data that survives SaveSystem.load() after a simulated reload.
testSteps: Clear save data
Call SaveSystem.recordStageCompletion('1-1', 45.5, 12)
Call SaveSystem.recordStageCompletion('1-2', 60.2, 18)
Reload save data via SaveSystem.load()
Verify completedStages includes '1-1' and '1-2'
Verify bestTimes['1-1'] === 45.5 and coinRecords['1-1'] === 12
Verify bestTimes['1-2'] === 60.2 and coinRecords['1-2'] === 18
criteria.threshold: Save data round-trips through localStorage correctly for completed stages, times, and coins

criteria.description: Save system correctly tracks best times (lower is better) and best coin records (higher is better). Re-completing a stage with a worse time and fewer coins does not overwrite the better records.
testSteps: Clear save data
Record completion: SaveSystem.recordStageCompletion('1-1', 30, 15)
Record again with worse values: SaveSystem.recordStageCompletion('1-1', 50, 10)
Load and verify bestTimes['1-1'] === 30 (kept the better time)
Load and verify coinRecords['1-1'] === 15 (kept the higher coins)
Record again with better values: SaveSystem.recordStageCompletion('1-1', 25, 20)
Load and verify bestTimes['1-1'] === 25 and coinRecords['1-1'] === 20
criteria.threshold: Best time only updates if new time is lower; coin record only updates if new count is higher

criteria.description: New Game / clear save works correctly. SaveSystem.clearSave() removes all save data, and subsequent load returns default data with empty completed stages.
testSteps: Record some completions
Call SaveSystem.clearSave()
Call SaveSystem.load()
Verify completedStages is empty array
Verify bestTimes is empty object
Verify coinRecords is empty object
Verify lives equals PLAYER_START_LIVES (5)
criteria.threshold: After clearSave(), load() returns default data with no completions and default lives

criteria.description: Coyote time is implemented: COYOTE_FRAMES constant is 6 (approximately 100ms at 60fps). Player can jump for 6 frames after leaving a platform edge.
testSteps: Verify COYOTE_FRAMES = 6 in constants.js
Search player.js for coyoteTimer usage
Verify coyoteTimer is set to COYOTE_FRAMES when player leaves ground
Verify coyoteTimer decrements each frame while airborne
Verify jump is allowed when coyoteTimer > 0 even if not onGround
criteria.threshold: COYOTE_FRAMES = 6, coyoteTimer set on leaving ground, jump allowed during coyote window

criteria.description: Jump buffering is implemented: JUMP_BUFFER_FRAMES constant is 8 (approximately 133ms at 60fps). Pressing jump before landing queues the jump.
testSteps: Verify JUMP_BUFFER_FRAMES = 8 in constants.js
Search player.js for jumpBufferTimer usage
Verify jumpBufferTimer is set to JUMP_BUFFER_FRAMES when jump is pressed while airborne
Verify jumpBufferTimer decrements each frame
Verify landing with jumpBufferTimer > 0 triggers an automatic jump
criteria.threshold: JUMP_BUFFER_FRAMES = 8, buffer timer set on air-press, auto-jump triggers on landing within buffer window

criteria.description: Variable-height jump is implemented. Releasing the jump button early cuts the upward velocity by VARIABLE_JUMP_MULTIPLIER (0.4), producing a shorter jump.
testSteps: Verify VARIABLE_JUMP_MULTIPLIER = 0.4 in constants.js
Search player.js for jump-cut or variable jump logic
Verify that releasing the jump key while vy < 0 multiplies vy by VARIABLE_JUMP_MULTIPLIER
This should produce noticeably different jump heights for tap vs hold
criteria.threshold: Releasing jump early multiplies upward velocity by 0.4, producing variable jump heights

criteria.description: Wall slide works: pressing into a wall while airborne triggers wall slide state with reduced fall speed (WALL_SLIDE_SPEED = 2).
testSteps: Verify WALL_SLIDE_SPEED = 2 in constants.js
Search player.js for wallSlide state handling
Verify player enters wallSlide state when airborne, pressing into a wall, and vy > 0
Verify vy is clamped to WALL_SLIDE_SPEED during wall slide
Verify wall jump is available from wall slide state (uses WALL_JUMP_VX and WALL_JUMP_VY)
criteria.threshold: Wall slide clamps fall speed to 2, triggered when pressing into wall while airborne and falling

criteria.description: Game runs at 60fps on initial load and title screen. The frame time tracking array in Game.frameTimes averages below 20ms per frame (50+ FPS) during the first 5 seconds.
testSteps: Load the game and wait 5 seconds on the title screen
Read window.__gameDebug.fps
Verify fps >= 50
Alternatively, read window.__gameDebug.frameTimes, calculate average of last 60 entries
Verify average frame time < 20ms
criteria.threshold: Average FPS >= 50 on title screen after 5 seconds of running

criteria.description: Game maintains adequate frame rate during stage gameplay. After entering a stage (e.g., 1-1) and playing for 3+ seconds, FPS remains above 45.
testSteps: Load the game, navigate to world map, unlock all stages
Enter stage 1-1
Wait 3 seconds for gameplay to stabilize
Read window.__gameDebug.fps
Verify fps >= 45
criteria.threshold: FPS >= 45 during active stage gameplay

criteria.description: Particle system has an upper bound to prevent unbounded growth. Particles.particles array length is capped (e.g., old particles are removed when count exceeds a limit, or particles have finite lifetimes).
testSteps: Inspect particles.js for particle lifetime management
Verify each particle has a lifetime/life property that decreases over time
Verify particles are removed from the array when their lifetime expires
Check if there's a maximum particle count cap
Verify the update loop filters/removes dead particles each frame
criteria.threshold: Particles have finite lifetimes and are removed when expired; no unbounded particle array growth

criteria.description: All 13 boss types match their spec hit counts. Boss maxHealth values are: Elder Shroomba 5, Vine Mother 6, Stag King 7, Sand Wyrm 6, Pharaoh Specter 8, Hydra Cactus 12, Frost Bear 7, Crystal Witch 25, Yeti Monarch 9, Lava Serpent 7, Iron Warden 7, Dragon 11, Architect 19.
testSteps: For each boss type, call Enemies.spawnBoss(type, 500, 300)
Read Enemies.boss.maxHealth
Compare to expected value from spec
Reset enemies between tests: Enemies.init()
criteria.threshold: All 13 boss maxHealth values match the product spec within tolerance (exact match or +-1)

criteria.description: All boss types can be spawned without errors. Enemies.spawnBoss() works for every boss type referenced in Level.bossData across all 13 stages.
testSteps: For each of the 13 stages, load the stage via Level.loadStage(id)
Read Level.bossData.type
Call Enemies.spawnBoss(Level.bossData.type, Level.bossData.spawnX, Level.bossData.spawnY)
Verify Enemies.boss is not null after spawn
Verify no JS errors thrown
Call Enemies.init() to reset between tests
criteria.threshold: All 13 boss types spawn successfully without errors

criteria.description: Ice physics are active in Tundra world stages (3-1, 3-2, 3-3). ICE_FRICTION (0.97) and ICE_ACCELERATION (0.04) constants are defined and used by the physics system when player is on ice tiles.
testSteps: Verify ICE_FRICTION = 0.97 and ICE_ACCELERATION = 0.04 in constants.js
Verify Tundra stages (3-x) have ice tiles (TILE_ICE) in their level data
Verify physics.js applyMovement checks for onIce flag and uses ICE_FRICTION/ICE_ACCELERATION
Verify resolveCollisions sets entity.onIce when standing on TILE_ICE
criteria.threshold: Ice physics constants defined, ice tiles present in Tundra levels, physics system applies reduced friction on ice

criteria.description: Auto-scroll mechanism exists for stage 3-3 (Avalanche Peak). The camera or level has an auto-scroll mode that continuously advances, and the player dies if left behind.
testSteps: Load stage 3-3 and inspect Level properties for auto-scroll flags
Search for autoScroll, autoScrollSpeed, or similar in level.js, camera.js, or states.js
Verify auto-scroll advances the camera or a kill boundary from left to right
Verify player death or damage occurs when behind the auto-scroll boundary
criteria.threshold: Stage 3-3 has auto-scroll mechanism that advances a boundary and penalizes the player for falling behind

criteria.description: AudioManager initializes without errors and all core sound methods exist: playJump, playLand, playAttack, playHurt, playCoinCollect, playStageClear, playGameOver, startAmbient, stopAmbient, startBossMusic, stopBossMusic.
testSteps: Verify AudioManager object exists on window
Check that each method is a function: typeof AudioManager.playJump === 'function'
Repeat for all 11 listed methods
Call AudioManager.init() and verify no errors
criteria.threshold: All 11 audio methods exist as functions on AudioManager and init() completes without errors

criteria.description: All game states are defined and reachable. GameState has constants for: TITLE, CONTROLS, WORLD_MAP, STAGE, PAUSED, GAME_OVER, STAGE_COMPLETE, VICTORY. Each state has both update and render handlers.
testSteps: Verify GameState.TITLE, CONTROLS, WORLD_MAP, STAGE, PAUSED, GAME_OVER, STAGE_COMPLETE, VICTORY are all defined as non-empty strings
Verify the update() switch statement handles all 8 states
Verify the render() switch statement handles all 8 states
Verify transitionTo() and changeTo() methods exist
criteria.threshold: All 8 game states defined with corresponding update and render handlers

criteria.description: Pause and resume works correctly. Pressing Escape during STAGE state transitions to PAUSED. Resuming from PAUSED returns to STAGE. The stage is still rendered behind the pause overlay.
testSteps: Inspect _updateStage for Escape key handling
Verify it calls changeTo(PAUSED) (instant, no iris-wipe)
Inspect _updatePaused for resume handling
Verify resume calls changeTo(STAGE)
Verify render for PAUSED state calls _renderStage(ctx) then Menu.renderPause(ctx) as overlay
criteria.threshold: Escape toggles between STAGE and PAUSED; stage renders behind pause menu overlay

criteria.description: No undefined references in the global scope. All global objects referenced in index.html script loading order are defined: Game, GameState, Renderer, Input, Level, Physics, Camera, Player, Particles, Enemies, Transition, HUD, Menu, SaveSystem, Collectibles, WorldMap, AudioManager.
testSteps: Load the game page
For each of the 17 global objects, verify typeof window[name] !== 'undefined'
Verify each is an object (not null)
List: Game, GameState, Renderer, Input, Level, Physics, Camera, Player, Particles, Enemies, Transition, HUD, Menu, SaveSystem, Collectibles, WorldMap, AudioManager
criteria.threshold: All 17 global objects are defined and non-null after page load

criteria.description: No JavaScript errors thrown during a full game flow: title screen -> world map -> enter stage 1-1 -> gameplay for 3 seconds -> pause -> resume -> exit to world map.
testSteps: Open browser console and monitor for errors
Load game (title screen)
Press Enter to start (-> world map)
Press Enter to enter stage 1-1
Wait 3 seconds of gameplay
Press Escape to pause
Press Enter or select Resume
Press Escape again, select 'Return to World Map'
Verify zero JS errors throughout the entire flow
criteria.threshold: Zero uncaught JS errors during the complete title->map->stage->pause->resume->map flow

criteria.description: HUD displays correctly during gameplay: health hearts (up to 3), coin counter, stage name, and boss health bar (when boss is active). HUD.render function exists and draws all elements.
testSteps: Inspect hud.js for render function
Verify it draws health hearts based on Player.hp and PLAYER_MAX_HP
Verify it draws coin counter reading from GameState.coinsCollected or Player.coins
Verify it draws stage name from GameState.stageName
Verify boss health bar rendering is conditional on Enemies.boss existing
criteria.threshold: HUD renders health hearts, coin counter, stage name, and conditional boss health bar

criteria.description: Collectibles system properly tracks coins during gameplay. Collecting a coin increments GameState.coinsCollected and the coin disappears from the stage.
testSteps: Inspect collectibles.js for coin collection logic
Verify collision detection between player and coin positions
Verify collected coins are marked as collected (removed or flagged)
Verify GameState.coinsCollected increments on collection
Verify coin collect triggers AudioManager.playCoinCollect()
criteria.threshold: Coin collection increments counter, removes coin from stage, and plays sound

outOfScope: Adding new stages, enemies, or boss types
Adding new movement mechanics or combat abilities
Changing the visual effects system (particles, shake, etc.)
Adding new game states or menu screens
Changing the rendering resolution or aspect ratio
